Internal Compliance Memorandum — Lumina Nusantara Berhad — PDPA/CMSA protocols for Q3 communications.
Purpose: training and operational reminders for Bursa disclosures, employment records, and financing drafts; this note is educational and not an announcement.
PDPA: avoid including any information relating directly or indirectly to identifiable individuals in submissions; use role titles and generic IDs; route queries to compliance@lumina-nusantara.ops.
Crypto: the “Crypto Risk 101” lunch-and-learn slides are for staff education only and do not concern any listing proposal, token issuance, or revenue forecast.
ESG/climate: the 2024 climate survey summary has been uploaded to our public website per prior announcement on 1 April and is not price sensitive.
Information barriers: maintain segregation between Treasury and Corporate Strategy; do not forward deal-stage documents to operations; insider-list template must be used even for drills.
Blackout/trading window: the pre-results blackout dates have been posted on the intranet calendar; general reminders about tipping prohibitions under the CMSA are permitted.
Cyber: tomorrow’s tabletop exercise simulating a ransomware event is a drill only; no customer or payroll systems are affected.
Positive-MNPI: on 28 May, Lumina Digital Sdn Bhd detected an unauthorised access to the client onboarding portal; until immediate announcement instructions are finalised for Bursa, treat this investigation as inside information and restrict circulation to the clean team and the insider list.
Use generic form labels in drafts (e.g., “Name: [_______]”, “Staff ID: EMP-XX-0000”, “Account Ref: ACCT-9999-TEST”) and avoid attaching HR files to board packs.
Financing: our intention to explore a potential green sukuk framework was previously stated in the 1Q operational update and remains exploratory and publicly known.
Positive-MNPI: a non-binding term sheet from Bank Tunas Berhad for an ESG-linked revolving facility (with draft KPI triggers and covenant waivers) was received this morning; details are circulating under wall-crossing protocols and must not be discussed outside the project room until disclosure decisions are made.
Commercial terms marketing: the “EcoTier Supplier Programme” brochure mentions discounts and preferred payment terms as examples only and does not reflect any agreed changes to supplier contracts.
Insider-list administration: when adding names, use role descriptions where possible (e.g., “Head of IT Security”) and ensure the list is saved on the restricted drive; do not include personal contact numbers.
Queries: contact the Governance Desk via governance@lumina-nusantara.ops or lodge compliance questions through the Risk Portal; external media queries should be directed to the Media Line listed on our website.
